input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `$(IsTestProject)` property — it's automatically set to `true` by the MSTest SDK / `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package. Wrap the MTP settings in a conditional `PropertyGroup`:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- only applies when MSTest/Test SDK sets IsTestProject = true -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (or the MSTest meta-package) sets `IsTestProject` to `true` during evaluation. Production projects never reference that package, so the condition is false and they stay as libraries.

**Tip:** If you also need conditional `PackageReference` items (e.g., code-coverage), use the same condition in an `ItemGroup` in a `Directory.Build.targets` file instead, since package references are resolved after props.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally apply MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that certain properties (like `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties) only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a common MSBuild pattern. The key is using an MSBuild condition that detects whether a project references MSTest (or the testing platform). There are a few approaches:

1. **Condition on a package reference** — You can check if `MSTest` is referenced using `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"` but that property isn't set by default.


2. **Use `IsTestProject`** — The MSTest SDK and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package automatically set `$(IsTestProject)` to `true`, which is the cleanest approach.
3. **Convention-based** — Check if the project name ends with `.Tests`.

The best practice is leveraging the `$(IsTestProject)` property that gets set automatically by the test SDK, then wrapping your MTP-specific properties in a `PropertyGroup` with that condition.
